name: Allgeyer v. Louisiana
question: Does a Louisiana law prohibiting out-of-state insurance corporations from conducting business in the state without maintaining at least one place of business and an authorized agent in the state violate the Fourteenth Amendment's Due Process Clause, which protects companies’ liberty to enter into contracts with businesses of their choice?
description: A case in which the Court held that the Fourteenth Amendment protects individuals from restrictions on their freedom to contract in pursuit of one's livelihood or vocation.
